reviews.text:
Across all three cases, the attorney was not collaborative and was unreceptive to feedback or strategic input. Effective legal representation, especially in a corporate context, requires collaboration with the client (us) and alignment with their objectives. This did not occur.

Furthermore, at no point was the attorney instructed to independently represent or speak on behalf of our clients without coordination. In corporate law, unilateral representation without alignment or authorization is a significant red flag and a clear breach of best practices. In hindsight, this should have been recognized as an early indication that the attorney was ill-prepared to serve as counsel for our matters. Taken together, these issues reflected a pattern of inadequate preparation, poor courtroom advocacy, lack of strategic collaboration, and delayed execution. Unfortunately, the level of representation provided did not meet the professional standards expected for our clients or our company. After the first case, I wanted my retainer reimbursed for the case not yet heard by the judge, but was denied by Attorney Cuyler and told my retainer would be kept, so I had to proceed with the others. I DO NOT RECOMMEND.

reviews.text:
I tried working with the VA on my own steam after my husband, a Vietnam War combat veteran, passed away in 2014.   That was a complete waste of time, and I was treated with anything but dignity.  The local VA was somewhat helpful, but the VA Evidence Center in Wisconsin were slow in responding, did not fully vet the information provided, and often gave conflicting responses which the VA Judge(s) had to return for more evidence.  At one point, I thought, "Is this a racial thing?"

After his review went all the way to the Bureau in Washington, with little to no compassion for his illnesses or me, the DAV put me in touch with CCK.  The experience has been daunting, but they never gave up or gave in.  They were gracious to me, but strategic with the VA.  They persevered even when I thought all efforts were hopeless.  Even though the VA is supposed to give the veteran or surviving spouse the 'benefit of the doubt', they did not and repeatedly denied my husband's CLEARLY substantiated claims.  He submitted his claims for assistance, starting in 2007, and it was THIS year, yes, 2025 (18 years later) that all was finally resolved. I received limited compensation for several of his claims along the way, but for several others, the answer was repeatedly no. CCK persevered, and I appreciate their work.  They stuck with me throughout the ordeal by snail mail, e-mail, and calls with updates, answers, and results! I highly recommend them if you have been staring down the VA barrel for a while; CCK is a sharpshooter, and they have several smoking guns to fight your battle(s). Believe me, you will need a law firm that has lawyers who are competent, feisty, and who won't become battle weary.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for sharing your story and your powerful words. We're truly sorry for the years of hardship and frustration you experienced, and we're honored to have stood beside you in this long and difficult fight for justice. Your perseverance, strength, and trust in us mean everything—and we’re so glad we could help you reach the resolution your husband deserved. Your experience speaks to the challenges far too many surviving spouses face, and we’re proud to have helped make a difference. Thank you again for your heartfelt recommendation and best wishes!

I am thrilled to share my experience with Attorney Sam Eno of Eno Eno and Associates, who represented me during my accident case in 2021. From the moment I engaged his services, it was clear that I was in capable hands. His steadfast dedication and unwavering support made a significant difference in navigating the complexities of my situation.

From our very first meeting, he demonstrated a genuine concern for my well-being. He took the time to listen attentively to my account of the accident, empathizing with the challenges I faced both physically and emotionally. His ability to connect with clients on a personal level immediately put me at ease, allowing me to trust him with my case.

Throughout the process, Attorney Eno remained unmovable in his commitment to achieving the best possible outcome for me. He meticulously gathered evidence, consulting with medical professionals to understand the extent of my injuries and how they would impact my life moving forward. His thorough approach showed me that he was not just interested in settling the case quickly but was dedicated to building a solid argument that would support my claims.

Communication is key in any legal matter, and Attorney Eno excelled in this regard. He consistently kept me informed about the progress of my case, explaining each step in detail and ensuring that I understood my options. Whenever I had questions or concerns, he was always available, promptly addressing my inquiries with clarity and patience. This level of accessibility was incredibly reassuring during such a stressful time.

In the end, the outcome of my case was favorable, and I attribute this success to Attorney Eno’s hard work and determination. He not only secured a settlement that met my needs but also helped me regain a sense of control and closure after the accident.

my experience with Attorney Sam Eno was outstanding. I wholeheartedly recommend him to anyone seeking legal representation, as I believe he will work diligently to achieve the best possible results. Thank you, Attorney Eno, for your exceptional service and support!

I never thought anyone in my family would ever end up in need of lawyers for personal injury but; after the None Emergency Medical Transport company we were using for my mom decided to play, flip the stretcher with her strapped to it in the middle of the parking lot that quickly changed. After the shock of what happened,  we decided to give Conley Griggs Partin a call. Right out the gate there was just a good vibe from everyone I talked to. Will Owens, Alex Edmonds, and Miguel Frederick were the ones from the firm that took our case, with Miguel being the main go between. At no point did any of them ever give the feeling my mom and her case was just a number on a spread sheet. That alone went a long way with us.

A little over a year later, they were able to get a very nice settlement from the insurance company which has given my mom and our family financial peace of mind, and that is after the firms 40% plus fees. So if you find yourself looking at this firm as an option and reading my review, give them a chance. Hopefully they will be able to help you too.

reviews.text: As soon as the representative answered the phone, I asked if they handled cases like mine. He responded that I would need to speak with an attorney to get an answer, and then revealed that he was just the receptionist. He then proceeded to ask me for all my information and a detailed description of my case. After I provided that, he abruptly told me that it was outside their scope of practice. I was taken aback - why didn't he say that from the beginning? I felt like I'd wasted several minutes of my time on a useless call with no helpful guidance.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate your call and apologize that you had to spend a few moments answering additional questions about your inquiry. However, we must clarify some points in your review. According to our intake team, we attempted to gather some basic information about your case, then promptly informed you that your inquiry was outside our scope of practice.  Our intake team also referred you to the local bar association. We take our case selection very seriously. Our mission is to provide the best possible representation for our clients, and that means we can only take cases in our areas of expertise. Sometimes, it is not immediately clear whether a case is one that our firm can handle. In those situations, we have to spend a few minutes gathering the appropriate information about each case inquiry. We hope you that you were able to find someone else to assist you.
